tasks.subject: [slack-adapter-world-class] [P1] Extract shared splitMessage() to payload-utils.ts
[slack-adapter-world-class] [P1] Create ThreadParticipationTracker
[slack-adapter-world-class] [P1] Add new config fields to SlackAdapterConfigSchema
tasks.description:
Update `packages/shared/src/relay-adapter-schemas.ts` to add the new config fields to `SlackAdapterConfigSchema`.

**Changes to `SlackAdapterConfigSchema`:**

```typescript
export const SlackAdapterConfigSchema = z
  .object({
    botToken: z.string().min(1),
    appToken: z.string().min(1),
    signingSecret: z.string().min(1),
    streaming: z.boolean().default(true),
    nativeStreaming: z.boolean().default(true),
    typingIndicator: z.enum(['none', 'reaction']).default('reaction'), // Changed from 'none'
    respondMode: z.enum(['always', 'mention-only', 'thread-aware']).default('thread-aware'),
    dmPolicy: z.enum(['open', 'allowlist']).default('open'),
    dmAllowlist: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
    channelOverrides: z
      .record(
        z.string(),
        z.object({
          enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
          respondMode: z.enum(['always', 'mention-only', 'thread-aware']).optional(),
        })
      )
      .default({}),
  })
  .openapi('SlackAdapterConfig');
```

**Key changes:**
1. `typingIndicator` default: `'none'` -> `'reaction'` (Improvement 3)
2. New `respondMode` field with `'thread-aware'` default (Improvement 4)
3. New `dmPolicy` field with `'open'` default (Improvement 5)
4. New `dmAllowlist` field with empty array default (Improvement 5)
5. New `channelOverrides` field with empty object default (Improvement 8)

**Update `SlackAdapterConfig` type** — it derives from the schema via `z.infer`, so it auto-updates.

**Backward compatibility:** All new fields have defaults. Only `typingIndicator` changes its default from `'none'` to `'reaction'`. Existing configs that explicitly set `typingIndicator: 'none'` are preserved.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Schema parses correctly with no config (all defaults apply)
- Schema parses correctly with explicit old config (backward compatible)
- New fields have correct defaults
- TypeScript types updated automatically via z.infer
- `pnpm typecheck` passes

tasks.subject: [slack-adapter-world-class] [P2] Add event deduplication to inbound handler
[slack-adapter-world-class] [P2] Add graceful auth failure handling
tasks.description:
Add fatal Slack error detection to `slack-adapter.ts` to prevent retry loops on auth failures.

**What to build:**

1. Add `FATAL_SLACK_ERRORS` Set at module level in `slack-adapter.ts`:
```typescript
const FATAL_SLACK_ERRORS = new Set([
  'account_inactive', 'invalid_auth', 'token_revoked',
  'not_authed', 'missing_scope', 'team_access_not_granted', 'app_uninstalled',
]);
```

2. Update the `app.error()` handler in `_start()` method:
- Extract error code from `error.code` and `error.data?.error`
- Check against `FATAL_SLACK_ERRORS`
- If fatal: log error, call `this.recordError()` with descriptive message, call `app.stop()`
- If non-fatal: existing behavior (just `this.recordError(error)`)

The `recordError()` method (from `BaseRelayAdapter`) already sets adapter status to `'error'` with the message, which surfaces in the Relay panel UI.

**Tests in `slack-adapter.test.ts`:**
- Fatal error code (e.g., `'invalid_auth'`) stops the adapter
- Fatal error in `data.error` field stops the adapter
- Non-fatal error is recorded but adapter continues
- Error message is descriptive (mentions re-checking bot token)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Fatal auth errors stop the adapter immediately
- Descriptive error message surfaces in Relay panel
- Non-fatal errors continue normal behavior
- No retry loop on token revocation

tasks.subject: [slack-adapter-world-class] [P2] Add respond mode gating and DM access control
tasks.description:
Add respond mode gating (Improvement 4), DM access control (Improvement 5), and channel overrides (Improvement 8) to the inbound handler.

**What to build in `inbound.ts`:**

After echo/bot/subtype filtering and after event dedup, add gating logic:

1. **Channel overrides check:**
   - Check `options?.channelOverrides[channelId]`
   - If `enabled === false`, skip with debug log
   - If `respondMode` is set, use it instead of global

2. **DM policy check:**
   - If channel starts with 'D' (DM) and `dmPolicy === 'allowlist'`
   - Check if `event.user` is in `dmAllowlist`
   - If not, skip silently (no rejection message)

3. **Respond mode gating** (non-DM channels only):
   - `'always'`: process everything (current behavior)
   - `'mention-only'`: check for `<@${botUserId}>` in event.text
   - `'thread-aware'`:
     - If `event.thread_ts` exists: check `threadTracker.isParticipating()` OR has @mention
     - If no `thread_ts` (main channel): only process if @mentioned

4. **Export `getEffectiveChannelConfig()` utility:**
```typescript
export type RespondMode = 'always' | 'mention-only' | 'thread-aware';
export interface ChannelOverride { enabled?: boolean; respondMode?: RespondMode; }
export interface EffectiveChannelConfig { enabled: boolean; respondMode: RespondMode; }
export function getEffectiveChannelConfig(
  channelId: string, globalRespondMode: RespondMode,
  overrides: Record<string, ChannelOverride>
): EffectiveChannelConfig {
  const override = overrides[channelId];
  return {
    enabled: override?.enabled ?? true,
    respondMode: override?.respondMode ?? globalRespondMode,
  };
}
```

5. **In `slack-adapter.ts`:** Pass config values through `InboundOptions`:
   - `respondMode`, `dmPolicy`, `dmAllowlist`, `channelOverrides`, `threadTracker`

6. **Parse dmAllowlist from textarea string:**
```typescript
const allowlist = Array.isArray(config.dmAllowlist)
  ? config.dmAllowlist
  : typeof config.dmAllowlist === 'string'
    ? config.dmAllowlist.split('\n').map(s => s.trim()).filter(Boolean)
    : [];
```

**Tests in `inbound.test.ts`:**
- Respond mode `'always'` processes all messages
- Respond mode `'mention-only'` processes @mentions, skips others
- Respond mode `'thread-aware'` processes DMs always
- Respond mode `'thread-aware'` processes @mentions in main channel
- Respond mode `'thread-aware'` skips non-mention in main channel
- Respond mode `'thread-aware'` processes in participating threads
- Respond mode `'thread-aware'` skips non-participating threads without mention
- Respond mode `'thread-aware'` processes @mentions in non-participating threads
- DM allowlist: allowed user processes
- DM allowlist: non-allowed user skips silently
- DM open policy: all DMs process
- Channel override `enabled: false` skips
- Channel override `respondMode` overrides global

**Acceptance criteria:**
- All respond mode variants work correctly
- DM policy gating works
- Channel overrides work
- All state is instance-scoped via options parameter
- All tests pass
tasks.activeForm: Adding respond mode and DM access control

tasks.subject: [slack-adapter-world-class] [P3] Replace Slack truncation with message splitting
[slack-adapter-world-class] [P3] Enforce thread-first responses and track participation
tasks.description:
Verify and enforce thread-first responses in all outbound code paths, and add thread participation tracking after successful outbound posts.

**Thread-first enforcement:**

1. In `outbound.ts` after `resolveThreadTs()`, add a safety warning for channel messages without threadTs:
```typescript
if (isGroupChannel(channelId) && !threadTs) {
  logger.warn(`outbound: no threadTs for channel message in ${channelId}`);
}
```

2. Verify `isGroupChannel()` is exported from `inbound.ts` (check if it's already exported; if not, export it). `isGroupChannel()` checks if channelId starts with 'C' or 'G'.

3. Audit `stream.ts` `handleTextDelta()` — the first `chat.postMessage` that starts a stream must include `thread_ts`. Verify the stream context carries `threadTs`.

4. Audit `approval.ts` `handleApprovalRequired()` — the Block Kit card must be posted in-thread. Verify `thread_ts` is passed.

**Outbound participation tracking:**

Add `ThreadParticipationTracker` parameter to outbound functions. After every successful `chat.postMessage` (standard, stream start, approval card), call:
```typescript
if (threadTracker && channelId && threadTs) {
  threadTracker.markParticipating(channelId, threadTs);
}
```

In `slack-adapter.ts`:
- Pass `this.threadTracker` to `deliverMessage()`, stream handlers, and approval handlers
- Clear tracker in `_stop()`

**Tests:**
- Channel messages log warning when threadTs missing
- DM messages don't log warning
- `markParticipating` called after successful post
- Stream start marks participation

**Acceptance criteria:**
- All channel messages use thread_ts
- Warning logged for missing thread_ts in channels
- Thread participation tracked on all outbound paths
- ThreadParticipationTracker cleared on adapter stop

tasks.subject: [slack-adapter-world-class] [P3] Update SLACK_MANIFEST configFields for new settings
tasks.description:
Add new configFields to the `SLACK_MANIFEST` in `slack-adapter.ts` for all new config options.

**New configFields to add (in an 'Access Control' section):**

1. **respondMode** — select with `displayAs: 'radio-cards'`:
```typescript
{
  key: 'respondMode',
  label: 'Respond Mode',
  type: 'select',
  required: false,
  description: 'When should the bot respond in channels?',
  section: 'Access Control',
  options: [
    { label: 'Thread-aware', value: 'thread-aware', description: 'Respond to @mentions and continue in threads the bot has joined.' },
    { label: 'Mention only', value: 'mention-only', description: 'Only respond when explicitly @mentioned.' },
    { label: 'Always', value: 'always', description: 'Respond to every message in every channel.' },
  ],
  displayAs: 'radio-cards',
}
```

2. **dmPolicy** — select with `displayAs: 'radio-cards'`:
```typescript
{
  key: 'dmPolicy',
  label: 'DM Access',
  type: 'select',
  required: false,
  description: 'Control who can DM the bot.',
  section: 'Access Control',
  options: [
    { label: 'Open (anyone)', value: 'open', description: 'Any workspace member can DM the bot.' },
    { label: 'Allowlist only', value: 'allowlist', description: 'Only users in the allowlist can DM the bot.' },
  ],
  displayAs: 'radio-cards',
}
```

3. **dmAllowlist** — textarea, shown when dmPolicy = 'allowlist':
```typescript
{
  key: 'dmAllowlist',
  label: 'DM Allowlist',
  type: 'textarea',
  required: false,
  description: 'Slack user IDs allowed to DM the bot (one per line).',
  placeholder: 'U01ABC123\nU02DEF456',
  section: 'Access Control',
  showWhen: { field: 'dmPolicy', equals: 'allowlist' },
}
```

4. **channelOverrides** — textarea (JSON):
```typescript
{
  key: 'channelOverrides',
  label: 'Channel Overrides',
  type: 'textarea',
  required: false,
  description: 'Per-channel settings as JSON.',
  placeholder: '{"C01ABC": {"respondMode": "always"}, "C02DEF": {"enabled": false}}',
  section: 'Access Control',
}
```

5. **Update typingIndicator** description to note new default: 'Enabled by default.'

6. **Update setupSteps[0].fields** to include new field keys.

**Tests in `slack-adapter.test.ts`:**
- SLACK_MANIFEST includes new configFields
- New fields have correct types and sections
- showWhen condition on dmAllowlist references correct field

**Acceptance criteria:**
- All new config fields appear in SLACK_MANIFEST
- Fields grouped in 'Access Control' section
- dmAllowlist conditionally shown
- setupSteps include new field keys

tasks.subject: [slack-adapter-world-class] [P4] Comprehensive test coverage and documentation
tasks.description:
Ensure all tests pass, add any missing test coverage, and update documentation.

**Test verification checklist:**

1. Run `pnpm vitest run packages/relay/src/lib/__tests__/payload-utils.test.ts` — splitMessage tests
2. Run `pnpm vitest run packages/relay/src/adapters/slack/__tests__/thread-tracker.test.ts` — tracker tests
3. Run `pnpm vitest run packages/relay/src/adapters/slack/__tests__/inbound.test.ts` — dedup, respond mode, DM policy tests
4. Run `pnpm vitest run packages/relay/src/adapters/slack/__tests__/outbound.test.ts` — splitting, thread-first tests
5. Run `pnpm vitest run packages/relay/src/adapters/slack/__tests__/slack-adapter.test.ts` — auth failure, config tests
6. Run `pnpm vitest run packages/relay/src/adapters/telegram/` — verify Telegram still works
7. Run full `pnpm test` to catch any regressions
8. Run `pnpm typecheck` to verify type correctness
9. Run `pnpm lint` to verify code quality

**Documentation updates:**

1. Update `contributing/relay-adapters.md` with:
   - New config fields: respondMode, dmPolicy, dmAllowlist, channelOverrides
   - Explain respond mode semantics (always, mention-only, thread-aware)
   - Note the typing indicator default change

2. Update `docs/relay-messaging.mdx` (if it exists and documents Slack config) with:
   - New respond mode options
   - DM policy documentation
   - Channel overrides format

**Acceptance criteria:**
- All tests pass across all affected packages
- No lint errors
- No type errors
- Documentation updated for new features
- No regressions in Telegram adapter
